name: adaptive-sentinel
hypothesis: cand-0008 achieves 0.975 composite with 237 DoD and 326 tests, but search-007 (notifications, hard) and search-011 (url-shortener, hard) both needed 2 rounds and had DoD misses. The adversary found gaps the planner missed despite negative requirements. An adaptive sentinel phase between BUILD and CHALLENGE can pre-scan for common gap patterns (missing edge cases in async code, missing error propagation in multi-module systems) and inject supplementary DoD items before the adversary runs. This should reduce retry rounds on hard tasks from 2→1 and push composite above 0.98.

changes.planner: Same as cand-0008 (deep planner with 5 DoD/feature + 2 negative requirements).
changes.sentinel: NEW PHASE: Post-build static analysis. Scans generated code for: uncaught async errors, missing input validation on public APIs, missing cleanup/teardown in resource-acquiring code, type coercion gaps. Emits supplementary DoD items (max 3 per task) as sentinel-dod.md.
changes.generator: Same as cand-0008 but also reads sentinel-dod.md on retry rounds for targeted fixes.
changes.adversary: Same as cand-0008 (skip covered negatives, go deeper). Now also reads sentinel-dod.md to avoid redundant challenges.
changes.evaluator: Same as cand-0008 but evaluates sentinel DoD items alongside planner DoD. Bonus for first-round completion of hard tasks.
changes.skill: 5-phase workflow: PLAN → BUILD → SENTINEL → CHALLENGE → EVAL. Sentinel adds ~2min overhead but should eliminate most retry rounds.
